title: Discrete Hazard Model Over Stint Survival
summary: The target is naturally a one-step event hazard: probability of stopping on the next lap given survival up to the current lap.
rationale: The target is naturally a one-step event hazard: probability of stopping on the next lap given survival up to the current lap. Discrete-time survival literature fits this framing directly and can enforce a cleaner notion of stop pressure than ad hoc tyre-window formulas.
implementation_hint: Expand each driver-race stint into person-lap hazard rows or approximate this with a classifier whose features are explicitly indexed by lap-within-stint and laps-remaining-to-finish. Include baseline hazard terms by `Compound`, `Race`, `Stint`, and `RaceProgress` bucket, plus interactions with degradation and lap delta. Convert hazards back to next-lap probabilities directly; if helpful, use the hazard model as a specialist or as a meta-feature rather than a standalone submission model.
expected_effect: May capture the shape of pit risk over a stint more cleanly than the current fixed service-life equations, especially when combined with grouped CV.
risk: This is close to prior hazard ideas, so gains may be incremental unless the baseline hazard is modeled explicitly and leak-safe; full row expansion can also add engineering overhead.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
